brief: Build a Python CLI tool csv2json: cli.py reads a CSV file path argument and prints a JSON array of row objects to stdout; a --pretty flag produces indented output; quoted fields containing commas and missing values must be handled correctly; include a pytest test suite under tests/ (verify with python3 -m pytest if available, else run cli.py directly on sample files); include a README.md with usage examples. Use only the Python standard library.

tasks.description: Implement the core functionality of the CSV to JSON converter in cli.py, including handling quoted fields with commas, missing values, and --pretty flag.

assertions.statement: Check that cli.py outputs valid JSON for a simple CSV
assertions.command: python3 cli.py tests/data/simple.csv | python3 -c 'import json; json.loads(input())'

assertions.statement: Check that cli.py handles quoted fields with commas correctly
Check that cli.py handles missing values in CSV

tasks.description: Create a pytest test suite in tests/ directory to validate all functionality

assertions.statement: Check that tests cover edge cases
Check that pytest test suite runs without errors

tasks.description: Create README.md with usage examples and instructions

assertions.statement: Check that README contains basic usage example
Check that README includes test instructions
